content: It’s easy to doubt the power of compound interest, especially when the gains seem slow at first. But here’s the truth: compound interest isn’t just about earning interest on your initial savings—it’s about earning interest on that interest, again and again. Imagine you put $1,000 in a savings account with a 5% annual interest rate. After the first year, you earn $50, making your total $1,050. The next year, you don’t just earn interest on the original $1,000—you earn it on $1,050. This means your money grows faster each year, not linearly but exponentially. Over decades, this effect can turn modest savings into substantial sums. It’s not hype; it’s simple math. The key is time: the longer you leave your money to grow, the more dramatic the increase. Skeptical or not, compound interest remains one of the most reliable ways to build wealth steadily.
